title: 'Limited time' requires an expiration
summary: AI-generated copy uses 'Limited time offer' without specifying the expiration, creating fake urgency that erodes trust when the offer never expires.
instruction: When using time-limited language, specify the actual deadline. 'Limited time offer' without an expiration date is fake urgency. Use a specific date, countdown, or quantity cap.
rationale: A 'Limited time offer' that never expires destroys credibility. Users who return to find the same 'limited time' offer weeks later learn the product's claims are unreliable. Specific deadlines ('Save 30% through March 31') create genuine urgency and respect user intelligence.

violations: 'Limited time offer' on a SaaS pricing page with no expiration date visible.
'Early bird pricing' with no deadline or expiration.
controls: 'Early bird pricing ends March 15: save 30% on annual plans.' with a specific date.
'First 100 customers get lifetime discount — 42 remaining.' with a quantity cap.
borderline: 'Limited time' in a perishable goods context (daily deals on fresh food) where the time limitation is intrinsic to the product.

exceptions.description: Clearance and liquidation sales where inventory is genuinely finite. Perishable service inventory (flight upgrades, hotel rooms) where time-limitation is intrinsic.
examples: Flash sale with countdown timer matching a real promotional calendar

detector.signalDescription: Find 'limited time', 'offer ends', 'expires', 'while supplies last' patterns. Check for nearby date, countdown timer, or quantity counter. Flag if urgency language exists without expiration evidence.
limitations: Expiration may be in fine print or legal terms rather than visible near the CTA.
Countdown timers rendered via JavaScript may not appear in static HTML.

scenarios: A flash sale page where the expiration is shown via a JavaScript countdown that isn't in static HTML.
A true limited supply (event tickets) where the time limit is the event date itself.
mitigations: Check for nearby date patterns, countdown CSS classes, or quantity indicators as alternative evidence.

copyPattern.replacementStrategy: Replace vague 'Limited time' with specific deadlines: 'Save 30% through [date]', 'First [number] customers get [benefit]', or 'Pricing increases [date]'.

fixturePlan.description: A SaaS pricing page with 'Limited time offer — save 40%' and no expiration date.
Same page with 'Save 40% through June 30, 2026' and a countdown timer.
A flash sale page with a JavaScript countdown timer but no static date text.
